title: Junta Airstrikes Continue Across Multiple Regions — 6 Civilians Injured
detail: On March 25, 2026, the SAC military continued its aerial bombardment campaign with airstrikes recorded in Nyaunglebin (Bago Region) and the Yaw region. At least six civilians were reported injured across these strikes. The attacks continued a pattern documented by CDM-affiliated media, in which the junta has sustained near-daily aerial operations against villages and townships in resistance-held or contested areas. The Progressive Voice civil society network reported that between February 26 and March 11 alone, more than 300 civilian fatalities resulted from aerial strikes — the campaign's most lethal sustained period since 2024.

title: Resistance Forces Fire 11 Rockets at Magway Air Base — Strike Hub Targeted
detail: At 11:20 PM on March 25, the Brave Warriors for Myanmar (BWM) and No More Dictatorship PDF (NMD-PDF) fired 11 rounds of 107mm rockets at Magway Air Base (BWM Mission 53). Magway Air Base is a primary strike hub for the junta's air campaign over Upper Myanmar and serves as a storage depot for KaPaSa 21 aircraft ordnance. The rocket attack — which is assessed as having impacted within the base perimeter — marks a continued resistance effort to degrade the junta's air power infrastructure. CDM media documented the attack with mission designation. The targeting of an airbase represents an escalation in resistance counter-air tactics.

title: NUG Representative Meets French Senate — Seeks Concrete Support
detail: On March 25, NUG representative Nan Su Mon Aung met with French Senator Pascal Allizard at the French Senate in Paris to discuss the ongoing Spring Revolution and options for practical international support. Senator Allizard had previously led a 2021 Senate motion calling for recognition of the NUG as Myanmar's legitimate government. The meeting reflects the NUG's continued diplomatic outreach to European partners as the junta pursues its 'civilian' transition designed to deflect international pressure. France has been among the European states more receptive to NUG engagement.
